homepage.heroSubline: Honest repairs for every make and model. Serving Memphis drivers since 2011 with fair pricing and reliable service.
aboutText: Legacy Auto Repair opened its doors in 2011 with three mechanics and a commitment to treating customers right. What started as a small shop in Midtown Memphis has grown into a 20-person team serving the entire metro area.
We believe in fixing cars properly the first time. Our technicians use manufacturer-grade diagnostics and only recommend necessary repairs—no upsells, no pressure.
Memphis roads take a toll on vehicles. We're proud to support local charities that provide transportation assistance to families in need.

valueProps.title: Same-Day Diagnostics
valueProps.description: Most vehicles get a full diagnosis within 2 hours—no waiting days for answers.

valueProps.title: Loaner Cars Available
valueProps.description: Free loaner vehicles for qualifying repairs over 4 hours.

valueProps.title: Memphis Weather Expertise
valueProps.description: Specialized knowledge addressing humidity-related corrosion and summer overheating risks.

valueProps.title: Upfront Pricing
valueProps.description: Written estimates before any work begins, with no hidden fees.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call or book online for same-week appointments at our Memphis shop.

services.name: Brake Repair
services.shortDescription: Complete brake inspections and repairs for all vehicle types.
services.description:
Memphis' stop-and-go traffic puts extra strain on braking systems. We perform comprehensive brake services including pad replacements, rotor resurfacing, and fluid flushes.

Our technicians measure wear patterns on all four wheels—not just the noisy ones. For Memphis drivers dealing with hilly neighborhoods like Evergreen or Central Gardens, we test brake performance on inclines during every service.

Common signs you need brake service: squeaking noises, longer stopping distances, or vibration when braking. We'll explain exactly what needs replacement and why.
process: Step 1: Road test and visual inspection of all brake components
Step 2: Measurement of pad thickness and rotor condition
Step 3: Hydraulic system pressure test
Step 4: Post-repair test drive to verify performance
benefits: Reduced risk of brake failure in heavy traffic
Smoother stops on Memphis' uneven roads
Extended lifespan of rotors through proper maintenance
Improved wet-weather braking performance

faq.question: How often should Memphis drivers get brake inspections?
faq.answer: We recommend every 12 months or 12,000 miles—whichever comes first. The combination of summer heat and winter moisture accelerates wear.

faq.question: Do you work on anti-lock brake systems?
faq.answer: Yes, our shop is equipped with ABS diagnostic tools required for modern vehicles.

services.name: Transmission Service
services.shortDescription: Preventative maintenance and repairs for automatic and manual transmissions.
services.description:
Memphis' mix of highway driving and city streets demands reliable transmission performance. We offer complete transmission services from fluid changes to full rebuilds.

For vehicles frequently crossing the Mississippi River via I-40 or I-55, we pay special attention to cooling system performance—overheating causes 90% of premature transmission failures. Our flush service replaces 100% of old fluid, not just what's in the pan.

Signs of transmission trouble include delayed shifting, slipping gears, or fluid leaks. Catching problems early often prevents costly rebuilds. We'll provide honest recommendations based on your vehicle's condition.
process: Step 1: Diagnostic scan for error codes and performance data
Step 2: Fluid condition analysis and pressure tests
Step 3: Road test to evaluate shift patterns
Step 4: Service or repair with OEM-approved fluids
benefits: Smoother acceleration from Memphis stoplights
Reduced risk of highway breakdowns
Improved fuel efficiency on longer trips
Extended transmission lifespan

faq.question: How often should transmission fluid be changed?
faq.answer: Most manufacturers recommend every 30,000-60,000 miles, but Memphis' hot summers may require more frequent changes.

faq.question: Can you fix transmission leaks without a full rebuild?
faq.answer: Many leaks stem from worn seals or gaskets that can be replaced individually—we'll always explore the most cost-effective solution first.

services.name: Electrical Diagnostics
services.shortDescription: Advanced troubleshooting for electrical and computer system issues.
services.description:
Modern vehicles contain more computing power than the Apollo spacecraft. Our ASE-certified technicians use factory-grade scanners to diagnose electrical gremlins that stump other shops.

Memphis' humid climate causes unique electrical challenges—corroded connectors, moisture damage, and battery drain. We specialize in tracking down intermittent problems that come and go. Common issues we resolve include dashboard warning lights, power window failures, and starting system problems.

For hybrid and electric vehicles, we perform high-voltage system safety checks before any diagnostic work begins. Your safety and our technicians' safety always come first.
process: Step 1: Full system scan with manufacturer-specific software
Step 2: Voltage drop tests on critical circuits
Step 3: Component-level testing of suspected problem areas
Step 4: Repair verification through extended road testing
benefits: Accurate diagnosis saves money on unnecessary parts
Prevention of roadside electrical failures
Extended battery life through proper charging checks
Faster repairs with our direct-to-factory diagnostic tools

faq.question: Why does my check engine light keep coming back after repairs?
faq.answer: This often indicates an underlying issue wasn't fully resolved. We trace problems to their root cause rather than just clearing codes.

faq.question: Can you fix electrical issues in older Memphis vehicles?
faq.answer: Absolutely—we maintain vintage diagnostic equipment for pre-1996 vehicles that don't have OBD-II ports.

services.name: Suspension Repair
services.shortDescription: Shock, strut, and alignment services for smoother rides.
services.description:
Memphis potholes and railroad crossings wreak havoc on suspensions. We restore ride quality with precision alignment and component replacements.

Our suspension services start with measuring ride height and checking all bushings and joints. For vehicles frequently parked on Memphis' sloped streets, we perform special angle compensation during alignments. Common repairs include ball joint replacements, shock absorber installations, and steering linkage fixes.

Notice any of these warning signs? Clunking noises over bumps, uneven tire wear, or the car pulling to one side. Left unchecked, suspension problems lead to premature tire wear and unsafe handling—especially during Mid-South rainstorms.
process: Step 1: Complete suspension inspection with weight measurements
Step 2: Road test to identify noise sources
Step 3: Component replacement using OEM-grade parts
Step 4: Laser alignment with Memphis road conditions in mind
benefits: Improved handling on Memphis' winding parkways
Reduced tire replacement costs
Smoother ride over railroad crossings
Better control in emergency maneuvers

faq.question: How often should alignments be checked?
faq.answer: After hitting any major pothole, or at least annually—Memphis roads require more frequent checks than flat cities.

faq.question: Do you work on lifted trucks?
faq.answer: Yes, we have alignment equipment capable of handling trucks with up to 6-inch lifts common in the area.

services.name: AC Repair
services.shortDescription: Cooling system repairs to beat Memphis summers.
services.description:
When Memphis hits 95° with 90% humidity, a working AC system isn't optional. We service all air conditioning components from compressors to evaporators.

Our AC specialists start by measuring system pressure and airflow at all vents. We pinpoint leaks using ultraviolet dye rather than just topping off refrigerant. Common repairs include condenser replacements (damaged by road debris), compressor clutch fixes, and cabin filter changes.

Don't wait until July to service your AC—spring maintenance prevents most summer breakdowns. We'll show you exactly which components need attention and which can wait, with no pressure to overspend.
process: Step 1: Performance test with temperature probes
Step 2: Leak detection using electronic sniffers
Step 3: Component repair or replacement
Step 4: Final charge with environmentally-approved refrigerants
benefits: Faster cooling in Memphis traffic
Reduced strain on your engine
Improved air quality inside the cabin
Longer component life through proper maintenance

faq.question: Why does my AC work sometimes but not others?
faq.answer: This usually indicates an electrical fault or intermittent refrigerant leak—we specialize in tracking these tricky issues.

faq.question: Can you convert older systems to new refrigerant?
faq.answer: Yes, we properly retrofit R12 systems to R134a following EPA guidelines, including all necessary component upgrades.

services.name: Pre-Purchase Inspections
services.shortDescription: Comprehensive used vehicle evaluations before you buy.
services.description:
Memphis' used car market offers great deals—and some hidden problems. Our 150-point inspections help you avoid costly surprises.

We go beyond basic checks to examine flood damage indicators (critical in our river city), frame straightness, and engine computer history. For trucks frequently used on Mississippi Delta farms, we pay special attention to undercarriage corrosion. Our written reports include repair estimates so you can negotiate intelligently.

Inspections typically take 90 minutes and can be done at our shop or the seller's location. We'll explain our findings in plain English—no confusing jargon—so you can make an informed decision.
process: Step 1: VIN check for accident history and title issues
Step 2: Complete mechanical inspection from bumper to bumper
Step 3: Road test including highway speeds
Step 4: Written report with photos and repair estimates
benefits: Avoid buying someone else's problem vehicle
Negotiation leverage with documented issues
Peace of mind about major systems
Expert assessment of repair costs

faq.question: Can you inspect cars at dealerships?
faq.answer: Yes, we frequently inspect vehicles at Memphis-area dealerships—just arrange access with the seller first.

faq.question: Do you check for flood damage specifically?
faq.answer: Absolutely—we know all the hidden indicators Memphis flood cars exhibit, including water lines in unusual places.

services.name: Routine Maintenance
services.shortDescription: Factory-scheduled service to keep your vehicle running right.
services.description:
Memphis drivers log more miles than average commuting across our spread-out metro area. Our maintenance services follow manufacturer intervals while accounting for local conditions.

We don't just change oil—our 30,000/60,000/90,000 mile services include thorough inspections of all critical systems. For vehicles parked outdoors in Memphis' heat, we pay special attention to rubber components and fluid breakdown. Our maintenance packages include multi-point inspections with digital photos of any concerning wear.

Keeping up with maintenance prevents 80% of roadside breakdowns. We'll help you prioritize services based on your driving habits and vehicle age—no cookie-cutter recommendations.
process: Step 1: Review service manual for your specific model
Step 2: Complete all scheduled maintenance items
Step 3: Inspection of wear items beyond the standard list
Step 4: Fluid analysis to catch problems early
benefits: Fewer unexpected repair bills
Better resale value with documented care
Optimized service intervals for your driving
Early detection of developing issues

faq.question: How often should Memphis drivers change oil?
faq.answer: Every 5,000 miles for conventional oil, 7,500 for synthetic—our hot summers break down oil faster than cooler climates.

faq.question: Do you service European imports?
faq.answer: Yes, we have factory-grade tools for BMW, Mercedes, and Volvo models common in East Memphis neighborhoods.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: Legacy diagnosed my transmission hesitation that two other shops couldn't fix. Turns out it just needed a proper fluid exchange—not the $2,800 rebuild others quoted. Their technician showed me the dirty fluid comparison and explained exactly how the flush process works. My Silverado shifts like new again.

reviews.name: Janelle K.
reviews.text: After my brakes started grinding near Overton Park, Legacy got me in same-day. They replaced pads and resurfaced rotors for half what the dealership wanted. What impressed me most was how they demonstrated the wear with measurements—no guessing. Loaner car was spotless too.

reviews.name: Robert W.
reviews.text: The inspection saved me from buying a flood-damaged truck advertised as 'clean.' The tech found water lines behind the dash and corrosion in the ECU connectors I never would've spotted. Detailed report helped me negotiate $3,500 off a better vehicle elsewhere.

reviews.name: Lisa M.
reviews.text: Fixed my AC before Memphis summer hit full force. They found a leaking condenser (probably from highway debris) and replaced it same-day. Only reason not 5 stars is the waiting area could use more seating during busy times.

reviews.name: Derrick S.
reviews.text: My Tahoe's power windows stopped working intermittently—Legacy traced it to a corroded ground connection near the door hinge. Fixed in under an hour when other shops wanted to replace the whole switch assembly. Honest work at honest prices.

reviews.name: Angela P.
reviews.text: Been taking my Camry here for 5 years. They always remind me when service is due without pushing unnecessary extras. Last visit they caught a cracked engine mount during the oil change—probably saved me from bigger problems down the road.

service-areas.title: Memphis Auto Repair Service Areas | Legacy Auto
service-areas.description: Legacy Auto Repair serves Memphis and surrounding communities with reliable vehicle maintenance and repairs.

serviceAreas.content:
Legacy Auto Repair is proud to serve the Memphis community with top-notch auto repair services. Whether you need routine maintenance, brake repairs, or engine diagnostics, our skilled technicians are here to help. We understand the importance of keeping your vehicle running smoothly in a bustling city like Memphis, where reliable transportation is key to your daily life.

Our shop is equipped with the latest tools and technology to handle all makes and models. From oil changes to complex transmission repairs, we provide honest and transparent service to ensure your car stays on the road. Trust Legacy Auto Repair for all your automotive needs in Memphis.
serviceAreas.localContext: Memphis is known for its vibrant music scene, historic landmarks, and bustling downtown area.

serviceAreas.content:
Legacy Auto Repair is your trusted auto service provider in Bartlett, TN. Our team is dedicated to delivering exceptional care for your vehicle, whether it's a quick tune-up or a major repair. We know how much Bartlett residents rely on their cars for commuting and family activities, and we’re here to keep them running safely.

With years of experience, our technicians can diagnose and fix any issue your vehicle may have. We offer a range of services, including brake inspections, tire rotations, and electrical system checks. Visit Legacy Auto Repair in Bartlett for reliable and affordable auto care.
